assumptions: Standard gfortran main entry stub; argc/argv are passed implicitly to _gfortran_set_args (the decompiler omitted the args because they are simply forwarded from main's registers).
`options.6.2` is the compiler-generated static options array; renamed to `options` for readability.
Stack adjustment (`rsp -= 8`) is a function-prologue artefact and dropped.
divergences: [low] other: Pseudocode shows _gfortran_set_args called with no visible arguments; rewriter adds argc/argv. This is the standard gfortran stub and decompiler elided the register-passed args, so semantically equivalent.
[low] other: Stack pointer adjustment dropped — standard function prologue alignment, no semantic effect.
[low] other: Symbol renamed for readability; assumed to refer to the same compiler-generated static options array.
